Pub. L. 105–354This amends subsections (f) and (g) of , as enacted by (, ). children.heading: .—General
children.label: a .—General
children.text: The board of directors is the governing body of the corporation.

The Administrator of the United States Fire Administration of the Federal Emergency Management Agency is an ex officio nonvoting member of the board. The Administrator appoints the voting members of the board.

children.label: C
children.text: one United States Government firefighter; and

children.label: D
children.text: nine individuals who have a demonstrated interest in the fire service.
The terms of office of the voting members are 6 years (except for the initial members). The terms shall be staggered so that the terms of 4 members expire every 2 years.
A vacancy on the board shall be filled within 60 days in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: .—Chairman
children.label: c .—Chairman
children.text: The chairman shall be elected by the board from its voting members for a 2-year term.

children.heading: .—Quorum
children.label: d .—Quorum
children.text: A majority of the current membership of the board is a quorum.

children.heading: .—Meetings
children.label: e .—Meetings
children.text: The board shall meet at the call of the chairman at least once a year. If a member of the board misses 3 consecutive meetings, that member may be removed from the board and that vacancy may be filled as provided in subsection (b)(4) of this section.
Appointment to the board shall not constitute employment by or the holding of an office of the United States.
Members of the board shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: .—Liability
children.label: g .—Liability
children.text: Members of the board are not personally liable, except for gross negligence.
